name: Sarah O'Brien
chief_complaint: Substernal chest pain with radiation to left arm, onset at rest 6 hours ago
pmh: Dyslipidemia (untreated — diagnosed 1 year ago, patient declined medication)
Family history: father had MI at age 45, paternal uncle died of sudden cardiac death at 52
ed_assessment: 38-year-old female with hypertension presenting with 6 hours of substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, onset at rest while watching television. Associated diaphoresis and nausea. Denies dyspnea. Describes pain as pressure-like, 8/10. Risk factors: active smoker, uncontrolled HTN, oral contraceptive use, strong family history of premature CAD, untreated dyslipidemia. ECG: sinus rhythm, no ST elevation, 1mm ST depression in V4-V6 and leads II, III, aVF. Troponin I rising: 0.8 on first draw, 2.4 at 3 hours. TIMI score 5/7 (high risk). Diagnosis: NSTEMI. Started aspirin 325mg loading dose, ticagrelor 180mg loading dose, heparin infusion per ACS protocol. Cardiology activated — cath lab discussion ongoing. Admit to CCU for monitoring and urgent catheterization planning. OCP should be discontinued.
handoff_notes: 38F NSTEMI, TIMI 5. Troponin rising (0.8 → 2.4). On heparin gtt — check PTT in 6h, target 60-100. Dual antiplatelet started (aspirin + ticagrelor). Cardiology at bedside — cath lab scheduled for 0700 tomorrow (early invasive strategy given TIMI score). NPO after midnight. Hold Lisinopril tonight (pre-cath). OCP DISCONTINUED — document prominently. If she develops chest pain overnight, immediate 12-lead and page cards. Patient very anxious — young patient, no prior cardiac history. Family (husband) at bedside.
baseline_functional_status: Prior to this admission: fully independent, no functional limitations. Works as a graphic designer (sedentary occupation). Exercises occasionally. Non-drinker. Lives with husband and 6-year-old daughter. Endorses high stress levels at work over past 6 months.

imaging.indication: Chest pain — ACS workup, evaluate for aortic pathology and pulmonary edema
imaging.findings: Normal cardiac silhouette. Clear lung fields bilaterally. No pleural effusion. Mediastinum not widened. No pneumothorax.
imaging.impression: Normal chest radiograph. No aortic pathology or pulmonary edema.

imaging.findings: Sinus rhythm at 94 bpm. Normal axis. 1mm ST depression in V4-V6, II, III, aVF. No ST elevation. No new Q waves. T-wave flattening in lateral leads. QTc 420ms.
imaging.impression: ST depression in lateral and inferior leads consistent with ischemia. No STEMI pattern. Serial ECGs recommended.

prior_hospitalizations.treatment: IV prochlorperazine, IV magnesium, IV DHE. Neurology consulted. MRI brain normal. Discharged on sumatriptan.
prior_hospitalizations.transitional_issues: Neurology outpatient follow-up. OCP discussed as possible migraine trigger — patient declined discontinuation at that time.
